economic_notes: First Nation owns 16.67% of Miitigoog Limited Partnership. Keniis Development Corporation is a fully consolidated subsidiary. COVID-19 uncertainty noted but no material revenue decline anticipated.
notes: Auditor name not included in the extracted text. Province inferred as Ontario based on Ministry of Community and Social Services and Ministry of Indigenous Affairs (Ontario) funding, Fort Frances Tribal Area Health Authority, and Grand Council Treaty #3 references. Keniis Development Corporation is fully consolidated. CMHC housing expenses include CMHC Rental Housing ($403,943) and CMHC Housing Renovations ($21,014). Health expenses include Community Care ($1,020,216), Health Programs ($41,647), Health Canada Integrated Services ($293,430), Aboriginal Healing and Wellness ($56,614) partially offset by prior-year figures; expenses_health here uses Health Programs + Health Canada Integrated Services + Aboriginal Healing and Wellness as direct health programs ($335,077). Multi-Unit Housing shows a negative expense of ($323,171) which is unusual, possibly a recovery or reallocation. No schedule of remuneration found in provided text. Net debt of ($1,956,778) reported. COVID-19 uncertainty disclosed.
